Every photo here is real

This is going to work.

It already is. Look.

Three kids who choose you.

Karston. Jeremiah. Maddy. Grown, laughing, and still showing up. That is not luck. That is a father they want.

Mom and Dad. Front row since day one.

A man who climbs actual mountains with you.

Love that laces up its boots. You didn't imagine David. You found him, and you got up the mountain together.

Cooper. Co-pilot.
And the latte is beckoning.

There will be down days. There always were.

And here is the record: you have come back from every single one. Undefeated. The downs are real. They are also outnumbered. Scroll back up and count.

Your own words hold here: a cascade, not a character flaw. It passes. You remain.

The diagnoses? Something. Not everything.

Five kinds of intense wiring. Aimed, they are the edge. Aiming is a skill. Every one has a lever, and you hold receipts.

  • ADHD Rocket fuel. The fire everyone else has to fake. Aimed, it builds whole worlds. Lever Interest, challenge, urgency. It never runs on should. Receipt An instrument for your whole life, built in a month of nights. Science Wiklund's studies: founders carry ADHD at multiples of the base rate, and it predicts speed to action. Hyperfocus is the documented edge.
  • OCD The fine edge. You build things true. Exactness nobody can teach. Lever Aim it at craft. Never at checking. Receipt The 18 edges you authored across your ten domains. That survey came from your hand. Science The error-monitoring circuits that loop are the same ones that catch what everyone else ships broken. Regulated, the lab calls it conscientiousness.
  • RSD Radar. You feel a room before it speaks. That is why they trust you. Lever Trust it outward, verify it inward. Read the room, never co-sign the sting. Receipt Clients hand you the real thing in the first hour. They can feel the radar caring. Science Dodson, who named RSD, is blunt about the flip side: the same antenna, aimed outward, is elite social intuition.
  • Anxiety The lookout. You see around corners. Prepared is your default setting. Lever Give it a real job. It writes the pre-mortem the Fortune 500 pays consultants for. Receipt You ask for the risk map before anyone else sees the reef. Every time. Science Norem's defensive pessimism research: worriers who plan perform with the best optimists. Worry, given a job, is strategy.
  • Depression Depth. Nothing about you is shallow. People feel that and stay. Lever Keep the depth. Never sign its verdicts. Receipt Fortify exists because your depth came back with a map. Client zero built the practice. Science Tedeschi's post-traumatic growth: people who return from the bottom measure higher in meaning, connection, and purpose. Lincoln ran a war on it.
  • The pit crew. Sleep, movement, Thursday, your people. Elite performers do not skip maintenance. They staff it.
  • The instrument. You do not white-knuckle working memory. You built Already to carry it. That is the executive move, not a workaround.
  • The clock. You schedule by capacity, not hours. The run, and the rest it earns. Your law, and the best coaching money sells.
  • The company. Branson runs on ADHD. Lincoln carried the melancholy. Founders carry this wiring at multiples of the base rate. The top is full of your kind of brain.

"Some days I can make the world turn with one thought."
Your words. Still true.

Still ahead, and already in motion.

  • Reckoning, on shelves. The book is real. Chapter one of the next gets penned in a London pub, David across the table.
  • The Sydney stage. Maddy's app on the big screen. Maddy at your side. Your story and your data holding the room.
  • The wild map. McCloud water. Bhutan temples. Everest is on the list because you put it there, and you meant it.

It works. It's fruitful. You're in it. FUCK YEAH. LET'S GO.

Say it out loud. Then go turn the world.

All of it real. All of it yours. July 2026
